channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Inside the ‘excellent’ Indonesian daycare where police found 53 children tied up and neglected
item.description: Indonesian police found 53 children tied up and neglected at a daycare in Jakarta, describing the conditions as "excellent" in a disturbing contradiction. The children were reportedly left unattended for extended periods.

item.title: U.S. says examining latest Iran proposal on Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister praised Russia ties and welcomed Moscow's support for diplomacy amid US tensions. U. S., Iran clash over Tehran's nuclear programme as review of atomic treaty begins at UN; Iranian Foreign Minister praises Russia ties, welcomes Moscow's support for diplomacy.

item.title: Mapping the destruction: How Israel ‘wiped out’ Lebanon’s Bint Jbeil
item.description: An Al Jazeera investigation mapped the destruction in Lebanon's Bint Jbeil, a town largely reduced to rubble by Israeli forces. The systematic demolition mirrors the devastation seen in Gaza. An Al Jazeera visual probe maps the systematic demolition of Lebanon's border towns, mirroring the devastation in Gaza.

item.title: When will Strait of Hormuz be ‘safe’ for commercial shipping again?
item.description: Shipping insurance costs for the Strait of Hormuz could increase 20 times over pre-war levels, analysts warn. Even if the strait reopens, the higher costs may deter commercial shipping. Even if the strait reopens, shipping insurance could cost 20 times more than before the war, analysts say.

item.title: White House to review presidential security; spotlight on line of succession
item.description: The foiled attack at the Washington Hilton hotel has prompted a White House review of presidential security. The incident also highlighted the presidential line of succession, with several cabinet secretaries present at the event.

item.title: How South Korea Uses A.I. to Check on Its Elderly
item.description: South Korea is using artificial intelligence to make care calls to older adults who live alone and to fight dementia in its rapidly aging society. The AI system aims to provide support to vulnerable citizens.

item.title: Algeria’s Luca Zidane doubtful for World Cup after chin and jaw fracture
item.description: Algeria's first-choice goalkeeper Luca Zidane suffered a chin and jaw fracture during a Spanish league match. He is doubtful for the World Cup due to the injury. Algera's first-choice goalkeeper collided with a player during a Spanish league match, breaking his jaw and chin.

item.title: Born during Israel’s genocide: Gaza’s child survivors bear the scars of war
item.description: Gaza's child survivors of the war face lifelong disabilities from toxic gas and burns. The healthcare system has collapsed, leaving them with lasting scars. Gaza’s youngest survivors face lifelong disabilities from toxic gas and burns as the healthcare system collapses.

item.title: U.S. no longer in position to ‘dictate’ policy to other nations, says Iran
item.description: Iran stated that the U. S. can no longer dictate policy to other nations. This comes as talks to bring a permanent conclusion to the conflict between Iran, the U. S., and Israel have been inconclusive.

item.title: German Chancellor Merz says Iran is humiliating U.S. as talks stall
item.description: German Chancellor Merz said Iran is humiliating the U. S. as talks stall. He noted that Iran is skilled at negotiating without achieving results. “The Iranians are obviously very skilled at negotiating, ⁠or rather, very skilful at not negotiating, letting the Americans travel to Islamabad and then leave again without any result,” says ​German Chancellor.

item.title: The Small U.K. Agency That’s a 911 for Ships in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A small U. K. agency operates a 24-hour service monitoring the Persian Gulf, Red Sea, and part of the Indian Ocean. It responds to distress calls from ships in the region, serving as a 911 service for vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 'We don't know what will happen to us': U.S. deportees in limbo in DRC
item.description: Fifteen South American migrants deported from the U. S. to the Democratic Republic of Congo face uncertainty. They have no ties in a country with ongoing armed conflict. 15 South American migrants and asylum seekers deported from the U. S.

item.title: The Process of Uranium Enrichment
item.description: is a complex industrial activity. It involves increasing the concentration of uranium-235 to produce fuel for nuclear reactors or atomic weapons. The Process of Uranium Enrichment.

item.title: 'US Defenseless Against Advanced Missiles': Pentagon's Big Admission Amid Iran War
item.description: The Trump administration is advancing plans for a comprehensive homeland missile shield. This move comes amid rising tensions with Iran and concerns over the U. S. defense capabilities. 'US Defenseless Against Advanced Missiles': Pentagon's Big Admission Amid Iran War.

item.title: Congressmen call for National Guard to address drone threat at World Cup
item.description: U. S. Congressmen are calling for immediate action to secure airspace over the 11 World Cup 2026 host cities. They want to address the threat posed by drones during the event.

item.title: Kashmir seminary declared unlawful under Indian law, sparks outcry
item.description: Indian authorities declared a Kashmir seminary unlawful, sparking outcry from local leaders. They view this move as part of a broader pattern of overreach by New Delhi. Kashmiri political and religious leaders denounced the move as part of a broader pattern of New Delhi’s overreach.

item.title: U.S. says examining latest Iran proposal on Hormuz
item.description: The US is examining Iran's latest proposal to lift the blockade on the Strait of Hormuz, a vital oil and gas shipment route. Iran had blockaded the waterway since the US-Israeli offensive began two months ago, impacting the global economy.

item.title: US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Trump Dissatisfied With Iran Proposal To Lift Hormuz Blockade
item.description: US President Trump expressed dissatisfaction with Iran's proposal to lift the blockade on the Strait of Hormuz. The proposal came amid ongoing ceasefire talks between the US, Israel, and Iran.

item.title: Hunt for 89-year-old suspect after gunman opens fire in Athens
item.description: A gunman opened fire at two locations in central Athens, wounding several people. Authorities are now searching for the 89-year-old suspect. A gunman has opened fire at two locations in central Athens, wounding several people, Greek authorities say.

item.title: German tourist dies in Egypt after snake crawls into pants, bites him during show
item.description: A German tourist died in Egypt after a snake bit him during a show. The snake had crawled into his pants, causing the fatal injury. German tourist dies in Egypt after snake crawls into pants, bites him during show.
